proven.claim: two‑assert pattern (= A B) and (not (= A B)) is present.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW] – Prove that isTrivialIdentity returns true exactly when the
; two‑assert pattern (= A B) and (not (= A B)) is present.
; We model the existence of the two required asserts with integer flags
; (1 = present, 0 = absent).  The function’s result is the logical AND of the flags.
; By asserting the negation of the intended property (result = 0) we expect
; Z3 to return unsat, proving the property holds.

;--- input model ---------------------------------------------------------
(declare-const eq_present   Int)   ; 1 if an assert of the form (= A B) exists
(declare-const neq_present  Int)   ; 1 if an assert of the form (not (= A B)) exists

;--- code‑transition model (isTrivialIdentity body) ----------------------
; The function computes: result = eq_present AND neq_present
; Encode AND as multiplication (since flags are 0/1).
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= result (* eq_present neq_present)))

;--- preconditions enforced by the function ------------------------------
; It first checks that there are exactly two asserts.
; Here we model that situation by requiring both flags to be either 0 or 1.
(assert (or (= eq_present 0) (= eq_present 1)))
(assert (or (= neq_present 0) (= neq_present 1)))

;--- assumption that the pattern is present (the “good” case) ------------
; The two required asserts are found.
(assert (= eq_present 1))
(assert (= neq_present 1))

;--- negation of the property we want to prove (i.e., assume result is false)
(assert (= result 0))

;--- check ----------------------------------------------------------------
(check-sat)
